creator: kp@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Andrey V. Elsukov from comment #14)
> ifa_free() does not free the memory immediately, so it is safe to make access to ifp->if_addr while you are in NET_EPOCH() section.

Right, but if ifa_free() queues the release to happen after everyone who might be using it has left the NET_EPOCH() section we can still have the pointer to a freed structure around if we don't NULL out the if_addr pointer.

The problem is that there are two pointers to the struct ifaddr. We remove one (from the if_addrhead list), but keep the other one around.
